You come across a rock with a strange glyph carved into it which looks almost like a rune. It glows as you approach.

Success: You recognise this as the mark of the Arcturian Society. A group of Rune Touched who teach Rune Magic.
Failure: It looks a bit dangerous.

You come across a rock with a strange glyph carved into it which looks almost like a rune. It glows as you approach.

Success: It takes some work but eventually you decipher the meaning. ```Those who visit the nexus between worlds would do well to be wary of stray foxes.```
Failure: It's very interesting but you can't understand what it means.

You come across a rock with a strange glyph carved into it which looks almost like a rune. It glows as you approach.

Success: It takes some work but eventually you decipher the meaning. ```Gestur Ironarm knew the truth but didn't tell the others.```
Failure: It's very interesting but you can't understand what it means.

You come across a rock with a strange glyph carved into it which looks almost like a rune. It glows as you approach.

Success: It takes some work but eventually you decipher the meaning. ```Arthur was gifted his legendary sword Justice by the Lady of the Lakes.```
Failure: It's very interesting but you can't understand what it means.

You come across a rock with a strange glyph carved into it which looks almost like a rune. It glows as you approach.

Success: It takes some work but eventually you decipher the meaning. ```The nexus connects to many worlds, simultaneously in all of them and in none of them.```
Failure: It's very interesting but you can't understand what it means.

You come across a rock with a strange glyph carved into it which looks almost like a rune. It glows as you approach.

Success: It takes some work but eventually you decipher the meaning. ```Rune Touched are not immortal.```
Failure: It's very interesting but you can't comprehend what it means.

You come across a rock with a strange glyph carved into it which looks almost like a rune. It glows as you approach.

Success: It takes some work but eventually you decipher the meaning. ```Many Rune Touched are lost and unaccounted for.```
Failure: It's very interesting but you can't comprehend what it means.

You come across a rock with a strange glyph carved into it which looks almost like a rune. It glows as you approach.

Success: It takes some work but eventually you decipher the meaning. ```Time is convoluted. Not all is as it seems. Watch the runes.```
Failure: It's very interesting but you don't vibe with it.

You come across a rock with a strange glyph carved into it which looks almost like a rune. It glows as you approach.

Success: It takes some work but eventually you decipher the meaning. ```tra-``` the message is unfinished.
Failure: It's very interesting but you can't understand what it means.

You come across a rock with a strange glyph carved into it which looks almost like a rune. It glows as you approach.

Success: It takes some work but eventually you decipher the meaning. ```-vel-``` the message is unfinished.
Failure: It's kinda interesting but you can't understand what it means.
You come across an ancient stone statue of King Arthur holding his legendary sword Justice.
You come across an ancient stone statue of Gestur Ironarm, a famous dwarven Rune Touched who fought a dragon long ago.
You come across an ancient stone statue of Bridgette Silverbow, a famous elven Rune Touched said to have wielded a legendary bow made of silver.
You come across an ancient stone statue of Merlin the Wizard, a famous Rune Touched said to have learnt magic in the earliest ages of the world. He was an advisor to King Arthur.
You come across an ancient stone statue depicting four heroes fighting a dragon.
You come across an ancient stone statue half buried in the ground. What you can make out looks like a fox.
You come across the remnants of an ancient burial ground. Countless skeletal remains lay arranged neatly across the floor of this cavern.
You cross paths with another travelers as you make your way through the caverns. They turn to you and say "Hey kid, wanna buy some death sticks?" They're selling a Longsword, +1 for 500 gp.
You see words scrawled in the wall of the cavern out of the corner of your eye```The Runes are watching```
A foul odour hits your nose, it smells like death.
You hear a loud crash echo through the caves. You don't know in which direction or what caused it. The only thing you know is it happened.
While you walk you smell something absolutely delightful.
You head down a narrow tunnel and come to a dead end.
The cave descends downwards into dark narrow tunnels.
It's soo dark and enclosed down here, you find yourself missing the sun and skies.
A barely perceptible breeze flows outwards from the tunnel ahead.
This cavern isn't quite short enough to crawl through but it isn't tall enough to stand up straight either.
You come to a narrow mineshaft whilst exploring the caverns. The walls and ceiling are supported by rickety wooden beams.
Three spiders hang from the ceiling of the cavern ahead.

The cavern you're exploring through comes to a sudden drop into the darkness below. The only way ahead would be to climb down, or you could turn back and find another way? Make an Athletics Check DC 10 to climb down or your next travel takes an additional `!enc`.
You come across a section of cavern where someone had once set camp. There's a black charred section where you think they had their campfire. Might be a good spot to rest.
A rat scurries past you in the darkness of the caves.
A bat flies past you in the darkness of the caves.
A spider scurries past you in the darkness of the caves.
You come across an area where rocks have been mined from the walls. You find a few traces of copper ore in the area.
You enter a large cavern filled with darkness. In most of the space the light from your torch doesn't reach the walls or ceiling. It makes you feel incredibly small.
You head down a narrow tunnel and come to a moss filled cavern. The ground is soft and cushioned and a mottley collections of green and brown colours.

The caverns around you are mostly silent except for a few distinct noises which acho and reverberate softly around you. The sounds of dripping water, your own footfalls and the clank of rocks as you pass them.
The way ahead is particularly narrow and requires you get down and crawl.
You can hear the sounds of something large and lizard like echoing through the cavern ahead of you.
You can hear the sounds of water dripping in a rhythmic pattern echoing through the cavern ahead of you.
You come across a small natural cavern. It might be a nice place to rest.
You come across a small natural cavern containing stalacites and stalagmites.
You come across a small natural cavern containing various sorts of mushrooms.
You come across a small natural cavern filled with countless spiders of all varieties.
You come across a small natural cavern with a bunch of sleeping bats hanging from the ceiling. They look kinda cute.
You come across a vast cavern, unimaginably large mostly shrouded in darkness save for a narrow beam of light that streams in from somewhere far up above.
You enter a chamber with a pool of water which drains out into an underground river. You can fish here.
You come across a cavern lined with glowing lichens and moss. The cave floor is lined with a blanket of flowers and herbs. You can forage here.
You come to a cave with a branching path. One direction smells foul, so you take the other route.
The cave is eerie quiet and dark save for a few scraps of glowing lichen, lining the walls.
A gnomish cross-section of damp passages and fungal growth lies trampled in the mud—field notes on air, drip, and spores. Someone catalogued this before you; cross-read with `!library field notes`.

Someone scratched tally marks beside Abyssal numerals near a side passage, as if matching disappearances to cave mouths. The pattern turns your stomach; archivists file such things under `!library horror`.
Dwarvish chisel marks ring a cracked pillar—warnings about stress, brace timber, and where the stone 'forgets.' A footnote cites deeper references—`!library dwarf`.
You come across a weathered stone shrine covered in moss. You can't make out which deity it was made out to but it leaves you with a sense of peace.

You come across an underground lake.

Success: You could probably do some fishing here. `!fish cave`.
Failure: It probably doesn't have anything interesting.

You come across a cavern filled with plants and mushrooms

Success: You could probably do some foraging here. `!forage cave`.
Failure: It probably doesn't have anything interesting.

The path ahead appears to dead end until you notice a rope hanging down one of the walls. You suppose you're meant to climb up...

Success: You climb the rope with little effort and continue down the tunnel at its top.
Failure: You didn't really want to go this way anyways. You head back to the last intersection.
You come across a hidden path that leads down to a crystal clear pond. The waters feel soo refreshing.
The moss in this cavern smells fresh and rejeuvenating.

You stepped on a tripwire, you only have a moment to react.

Success: You nimbly dodge out of the way.
Failure: You get stung by darts for piercing damage.

The rocks above you in the cavern ceiling start to shift, you only have a moment to react.

Success: You nimbly dodge out of the way.
Failure: Rocks fall on you from above for bludgeoning damage.

You come across a collapsed manmade cavern with glyphs and engravings across the remaining walls.

A few old Westmarch runes shimmer here, left as a narrative reward for servers that track them.

A collection of faintly glimmering runes appear in the air infront of your eyes.

A few old Westmarch runes shimmer here, left as a narrative reward for servers that track them.

Glimmering runes floating in the air lead you to a peaceful cavern.

A few old Westmarch runes shimmer here, left as a narrative reward for servers that track them.

Glimmering runes floating in the air mark the way before you. You wonder where they're leading you.

A few old Westmarch runes shimmer here, left as a narrative reward for servers that track them.

A swarm of bats fly overhead, filling the cave with sounds of flapping leathery wings. After they're gone you see a few runes floating where they were.

A few old Westmarch runes shimmer here, left as a narrative reward for servers that track them.

A curious rat pauses to sniff the air as it runs passed you. After it runs off you can see a rune floating where it had stopped to sniff.

A few old Westmarch runes shimmer here, left as a narrative reward for servers that track them.
You come across the body of a dead goblin. They have a few gold in their pockets.
You come across a cavern where water trickles in to form a basin against one of the walls. Besides the basin is a dusty old coinpurse.

You come across a discarded scroll case.

Success: You find a Scroll of Darkvision within.
Failure: It's empty.

You come across a discarded scroll case.

Success: You find a Scroll of Stone Shape within.
Failure: It's empty.

You come across a discarded scroll case.

Success: You find a Scroll of Detect Thoughts within.
Failure: It's empty.

You come across an abandoned, intricately carved and decorative stone statue of an interesting figure.

Success: It's a masterwork handcarved statue of you, in perfect detail.
Failure: It's cracked and crumbles to bits as you pick it up.

You come across a rock with a strange glyph carved into it which looks almost like a rune. It glows as you approach.

Success: The runes suggest a trade, half your life force for 25 Runes. If you accept you may add the runes but also take half your maximum hp damage and gain a level of exhaustion.
Failure: It looks a bit dangerous.

You come across a rock with a strange glyph carved into it which looks almost like a rune. It glows as you approach.

Success: The runes acknowledge your will and self determination. It offers you a trade, 5 Runic Shards for 15 Runes.
Failure: You runes stop glowing as you approach.

You come across a rock with a strange glyph carved into it which looks almost like a rune. It glows as you approach.

Success: The runes acknowledge your intellect and wit. They offer you a trade, 5 Runic Shards for 17 Runes.
Failure: Huh, it disappeared? Weird.

You come across a rock with a strange glyph carved into it which looks almost like a rune. It glows as you approach.

Success: Acting on gut instrinct you jump backwards. A split-second later the runes explode.
Failure: You get blasted as the rune explodes for force damage.
As you pass through the narrow stone corridor, faint whispers seem to trail just behind you. When you stop, they stop too.
A sudden chill wind brushes past you from deeper within the cave, carrying with it the scent of damp stone and something metallic.
Your torchlight flickers wildly despite the still air, casting long distorted shadows that seem to move on their own.
Half buried in the dirt lies a rusted iron helm, cracked cleanly down the center as though split by tremendous force.
Deep claw marks rake across the cavern wall here, the stone torn apart as if it were soft clay.
For a moment you hear footsteps that don't match your own pace. When you turn, the cavern is empty.
In a quiet alcove rests a rotted bedroll and a shattered lantern, long abandoned by whoever once sheltered here.
Jagged stalagmites rise from the ground like the teeth of some enormous beast waiting to snap shut.
You find a still pool of black water. Its surface reflects nothing, not even your torchlight.
A low roar echoes faintly through the stone, so distant you can't tell if it was real or your imagination.
A thin vein of pale crystal runs along the cave wall, catching the light and scattering it in faint rainbow shards.
A once-passable tunnel has completely collapsed, forcing you to turn back or find another route.
Pebbles tumble from the ceiling and clatter around your boots, though you see no cause.
A rhythmic drumming hums through the stone beneath your feet, like a distant heartbeat.
A small pile of bones lies in a circle of soot, as if something was burned here long ago.
The cavern splits into three uneven tunnels, each swallowed by darkness.
Tiny mineral deposits sparkle overhead like a frozen night sky.
A naturally formed archway of stone frames the tunnel ahead like an entrance to something important.
You step into a chamber so silent it feels oppressive, your own breathing suddenly too loud.
An old pickaxe leans against the wall, its wooden handle splintered and worn smooth by time.
A torn backpack spills its long-rotted contents across the cavern floor.
A thin mist clings low to the cavern floor, swirling gently around your boots.
A broken stone face lies on the ground, its expression frozen in solemn calm.
The stone beneath your palm feels unexpectedly warm, almost pulsing faintly.
Clusters of pale fungi glow softly along the walls, casting an eerie ambient light.
A thrown pebble falls for several seconds before striking something far below.
Lengths of rusted chain hang from iron rings bolted into the cavern wall.
A drifting cloud of dust swirls in the air as if something recently passed through.
An arrow symbol has been carved into the stone, pointing deeper into the cave.
Sections of the cavern wall appear melted and fused together, as though subjected to intense heat.
A deep fissure splits the cavern floor, too wide to step across comfortably.
Mineral-rich water drips steadily into a small stone bowl carved by time.
A shattered shield bearing an unrecognizable crest lies abandoned here.
The ceiling lowers drastically, forcing you to hunch as you proceed.
Faded writing in an unknown script stretches across the stone wall.
A neat arrangement of bleached bones rests against the cave wall.
For a brief second your shadow seems to move independently of your body.
A small carved niche in the wall looks like it once held something important.
Your breath fogs heavily in this chamber despite the otherwise mild air.
A pebble rolls slowly across the cavern floor from the darkness ahead.
A low continuous hum vibrates faintly through the stone around you.
The remains of a mining cart lie twisted and overturned.
The shadows here feel thicker than darkness should allow.
The air grows heavy and stale, as though untouched for centuries.
Natural stone pillars rise from floor to ceiling, forming a silent forest of rock.
A bent iron hook hangs from the ceiling where a lantern once swung.
The cavern floor shifts to soft sand, muting your footsteps completely.
A stone hammer rests atop a flat rock like an offering.
Cold water fills the lower half of the passage ahead.
For a brief hopeful moment, you think you see natural light far ahead.
Even the usual dripping water is absent here.
The tunnel slopes downward over loose rubble and broken stone.
The tunnel narrows into sharp jagged stone edges that scrape at your gear.
A natural formation resembles a crude stone throne at the chamber's center.
A ring of carefully placed stones surrounds the ash of a long-dead fire.
A small charm made of bone and twine hangs from a stalactite.
A thin crack in the wall seems just wide enough to squeeze through.
A thin waterfall cascades from high above into a shallow rocky basin.
The snapped haft of a spear juts from between two stones.
Something metallic glints faintly from beneath a layer of dust.
Tattered cloth hangs from the wall, its symbol faded beyond recognition.
This chamber is perfectly circular, almost unnaturally so.
Fresh-looking footprints trail ahead into the dark.
A small pile of deliberately stacked stones stands in the center of the passage.
A few dark feathers lie scattered across the cavern floor.
One wall is scorched black in a wide arc pattern.
A shallow icy pool blocks part of the cavern path.
A deep alcove in the wall looks large enough to hide within.
Hairline fractures spiderweb across the cavern floor.
A shattered stone idol lies toppled and broken.
Your voice echoes back slightly delayed and distorted.
A narrow natural bridge of stone spans a deep dark chasm.
Wet footprints lead away from a shallow puddle.
A faint glow leaks from a crack in the rock face.
A wooden crate sits unopened in the center of the chamber.
The path narrows to a precarious ledge hugging the cavern wall.
Wind whistles eerily through a tight rock passage.
Dozens of tall stalagmites block the direct path forward.
One section of the cavern is unnaturally cold compared to the rest.
A few scattered coins lie forgotten in the dirt.
A cracked wooden support beam groans softly under unseen pressure.
The remains of a recent rockfall litter the tunnel floor.
A flicker of light flashes in the far distance, then vanishes.
You feel as though something unseen is watching from the darkness.
A vast underground lake stretches out before you into blackness.
Rough stone steps carved long ago descend deeper underground.
A faded mural decorates the wall, too worn to clearly interpret.
A skeleton slumps against the wall, undisturbed for ages.
The ground here looks disturbed, as if something was buried long ago.
A faint draft suggests a hidden passage nearby.
The tunnel slopes sharply downward into pitch darkness.
Soft blue mushrooms line the cavern wall, releasing a faint soothing glow. Resting near them eases your strain.
Giant Subterranean Lizard, Rat, Bat, Spider ambush
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Giant Subterranean Lizard, Rat, Bat, Spider.
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Lizardfolk.
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Goblin.
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Skeleton.
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Zombie.
A hidden snare or terrain hazard signals an ambush by Lizardfolk.
It's quiet... too quiet. Without warning, a bulette comes crashing through the cavern wall, chomping down on you and looking to fight!

You see markings on the cave walls and floor which catch your attention.

Success: The signs of Lizardfolk in the area. You very wisely find an alternative route rather than bother them.
Failure: It's beyond you. Headed further into the cave you are ambushed by a crowd of Lizardfolk! One of them throws a net **restraining** you before you have a chance to react!

You see markings on the cave walls and floor which catch your attention. As you stop to take a closer look a swarm of undead lead by a Wight enter the room. You have but a moment to hide.

Success: They pass you by after a few minutes without noticing you.
Failure: They pass pass you by after a few minutes without noticing you... you think. The Wight stops right before leaving the cavern and suddenly turns to look right at you. You've been spotted!

You see markings on the cave walls and floor which catch your attention. As you stop to take a closer look a swarm of undead lead by a Mummy enter the room. You have but a moment to hide.

Success: They pass you by after a few minutes without noticing you.
Failure: They pass pass you by after a few minutes without noticing you... you think. The Mummy stops right before leaving the cavern and suddenly turns to look right at you. You've been spotted!
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Gray Ooze.
A hidden snare or terrain hazard signals an ambush by Gray Ooze.

The stone ahead glistens with a damp sheen.

Success: You notice the subtle movement in the rock and avoid the corrosive ooze.
Failure: It's beyond you. As you step forward, the stone lashes out—an ooze engulfs your gear, using it's **Corrode Metal** on all your equipped weapons!
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Purple Worm.

The ground trembles faintly beneath your feet.

Success: You recognise the signs of something massive below and turn back.
Failure: It's beyond you. The ground erupts as a purple worm bursts forth, attempting to swallow you whole! Run `!ma "Purple Worm" Bite -t "Your PC" before combat begins.`
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Earth Elemental.
A hidden snare or terrain hazard signals an ambush by Earth Elemental.

Loose stones shift strangely along the cavern wall.

Success: You sense an elemental's presence and give the area a wide berth.
Failure: It's beyond you. The rock itself rises and strikes, an earth elemental emerging from the wall!
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Goblin.
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Goblin.
A hidden snare or terrain hazard signals an ambush by Goblin.
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Phase Spider, Spider.
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Phase Spider, Giant Spider.

Strands of webbing cling to the stone, though nothing is visible.

Success: You notice the distortion in the air and avoid the lurking predator.
Failure: It's beyond you. A phase spider flickers into view beside you, its bite delivering the **poisoned** condition (DC 15 Con Save to remove at the end of your turn)!
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Zombie, Ghoul.
A hidden snare or terrain hazard signals an ambush by Zombie, Ghoul.
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Bulette.

The ground ahead is churned and broken.

Success: You recognise the signs of a burrowing predator and avoid the area.
Failure: It's beyond you. The ground bursts open as a bulette lunges upward, knocking you **prone**!
A hidden snare or terrain hazard signals an ambush by Bulette.
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Goblin.
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Goblin.
A hidden snare or terrain hazard signals an ambush by Goblin.
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Spider, Giant Spider.
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Spider, Giant Spider.

Fine strands of webbing stretch across the cavern passage.

Success: You spot the webs in time and carefully avoid the nest ahead.
Failure: It's beyond you. You walk straight into the webbing as spiders descend around you!
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Kobold.
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Kobold.

Scratched markings and small footprints dot the cave floor.

Success: You identify kobold signs and slip past their territory.
Failure: It's beyond you. Kobolds emerge from hidden tunnels, hurling javelins and flanking you!
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Swarm of Rats.
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Swarm of Rats.

A chittering echoes through the dark as something stirs in the debris.

Success: You avoid the swarm by keeping to higher, dry stone.
Failure: It's beyond you. A writhing mass of rats pours from the shadows, swarming over your legs!
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Goblin.
A hidden snare or terrain hazard signals an ambush by Goblin.
A hidden snare or terrain hazard signals an ambush by Goblin.

Faint chatter echoes through a side tunnel.

Success: You notice goblin tracks and avoid their patrol route.
Failure: It's beyond you. Goblins burst from the tunnels, surrounding you quickly!

Scratched markings and discarded scraps mark recent passage.

Success: You interpret goblin signs and slip past unseen.
Failure: It's beyond you. Hidden goblins trigger a cave-in and rush you from both sides!
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Spider, Giant Spider.
A hidden snare or terrain hazard signals an ambush by Spider, Giant Spider.
A hidden snare or terrain hazard signals an ambush by Spider, Giant Spider.

Webbing clings to the ceiling in thick, silent sheets.

Success: You spot the webbed tunnels and avoid the nest.
Failure: It's beyond you. You step into a webbed corridor as spiders descend from above!

The air grows still, strands of silk drifting faintly.

Success: You navigate around the nesting grounds safely.
Failure: It's beyond you. You disturb a hidden nest and spiders flood the passage!
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Kobold.
A hidden snare or terrain hazard signals an ambush by Kobold.
A hidden snare or terrain hazard signals an ambush by Kobold.

Small tunnels branch off the main cave, reinforced with crude supports.

Success: You identify kobold engineering and avoid their traps.
Failure: It's beyond you. Kobolds collapse the tunnel behind you and attack from hidden alcoves!

Success: You spot kobold ambush points before entering.
Failure: It's beyond you. Kobolds spring out from concealed tunnels, striking from all sides!
Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Swarm of Rats.

Success: You steer clear of the nesting grounds.
Failure: It's beyond you. A seething mass of rats erupts from the refuse and swarms your legs!

Chittering grows louder as shadows shift near scattered remains.

Success: You notice movement early and avoid the swarm.
Failure: It's beyond you. The ground comes alive as rats surge from every crack and crevice!
